anchors.9-10: No errors; polished professional register throughout
anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; fully intelligible; appropriate register
anchors.5-6: Some errors that slightly undermine professionalism
anchors.3-4: Frequent errors; noticeable impact on impression

anchors.9-10: Clinical examples are accurate, specific, and evidence-informed
anchors.7-8: Mostly accurate; minor imprecision in clinical details
anchors.5-6: Some clinical inaccuracies or vague clinical references
anchors.1-2: Significant clinical errors that raise concern

anchors.9-10: Outstanding composure, self-awareness, and ethical reasoning throughout
anchors.7-8: Consistently professional; minor lapses in composure or framing
anchors.5-6: Generally professional but occasional inappropriate framing or overconfidence
anchors.3-4: Several lapses in professionalism or self-awareness

anchors.9-10: Smooth, confident, natural delivery; appropriate pacing and signposting
anchors.5-6: Noticeable hesitation or filler words; some impact on impression
anchors.3-4: Frequent hesitation or filler; significantly reduces impact
anchors.1-2: Communication severely disrupted by fluency issues

checklist.item: STAR format used for behavioral question (Situation, Task, Action, Result)
specific concrete examples provided (not vague generalities)
genuine and specific motivation for internal medicine articulated
IMG background addressed directly and confidently if relevant
appropriate professional and enthusiastic tone maintained
questions answered fully without going off-topic
